narration: A sliding window is a moving section of the array.
callout: Here the first window of size 3 is [2, 1, 5].
decision: The window shows only part of the data at one time.

narration: When the window slides right, one value leaves and one new value enters.
decision: That is why we can update only what changed instead of rebuilding everything.

predictionPrompt.prompt: If the problem always says size k, which window style fits best?

choices.label: Fixed-size window
choices.feedback: Correct. The width stays the same.

choices.label: Dynamic window
choices.feedback: Not this one. Dynamic windows change width.

narration: Some windows stay the same width, and some grow and shrink as the rule changes.
callout: Fixed windows care about exact width. Dynamic windows care about whether the current section is valid.
decision: Choosing the right window style is the first big step in the chapter.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why can sliding windows be faster than brute force?
